direct_side_transfer: Use the corresponding side marks in the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the matching sides in the two similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the missing corresponding side length in the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Based on the visible corresponding side labels, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the similarity scale shown by the marked sides. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
single: Use the corresponding side marks in the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the matching sides in the two similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the missing corresponding side length in the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Based on the visible corresponding side labels, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the similarity scale shown by the marked sides. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
two_pair_side_transfer: Use the marked pair of corresponding sides to transfer the scale. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
The similar figures show one complete side pair and one missing side. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the scale from the visible corresponding side pair, then find the length of "{target_name}".
Use the two marked side pairs in the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Based on the corresponding side marks and lengths, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
nested_side_transfer: Use the nested similar figures and corresponding side labels. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
In the nested similar figures, transfer the side scale to find "{target_name}".
Find the missing side length in the nested similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the visible nested side relationship. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Based on the corresponding sides in the nested figures, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_area_pair: Use the area labels to get the scale between the similar figures. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the linear scale from the two areas, then find "{target_name}".
Based on the visible area labels and corresponding side, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the similar-figure area relationship. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Transfer the side length using the area scale. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_area_ratio: Use the shown area ratio to get the linear scale. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the side scale from the area ratio, then find "{target_name}".
Based on the visible area ratio and source side, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the similar-figure area-ratio relationship. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Transfer the side length using the displayed area ratio. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_area_and_known_side: Use the area labels and corresponding side label. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the linear scale from the areas, then transfer the side length to "{target_name}".
In the nested similar figures, use the areas and source side. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Based on the visible area scale and side label, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
Use the nested similar-figure area relationship. What is the length of "{target_name}"?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description: two similar figures with corresponding side marks and measurement labels
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with keys "answer" and "annotation".
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with key "answer".
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON object mapping each required label ({annotation_keys}) to one pixel point [x,y]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_integer_length: set "answer" to the requested side length as an integer
